target.title: fix: use explicit agent workspace when writing transcript headers
target.body:
## Summary
- stop deriving transcript header `cwd` from `process.cwd()` and resolve the target agent workspace explicitly before appending mirrored transcript entries
- add a regression test covering per-agent cwd isolation for transcript header creation
- cover the Matrix session-binding registry entry in contract tests and install the Matrix-specific harness inside the test file instead of the production registry
- replace a transparent Matrix credentials dynamic import with a static import so build-smoke, install-smoke, and gateway watch builds don't trip the ineffective dynamic import check

## Verification
- `pnpm vitest run src/channels/plugins/contracts/registry.contract.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/session-binding.contract.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts`
- `pnpm oxlint --type-aware src/channels/plugins/contracts/registry.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/session-binding.contract.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/suites.ts src/config/sessions/transcript.ts src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm build:docker`
- `pnpm test:gateway:watch-regression`

## Notes
- The production-facing fix is still narrowly scoped to transcript header cwd isolation.
- The Matrix contract harness and static import follow-ups are included to keep the repository's broad CI suites green on this branch.
- This PR supersedes #50099.

Closes #49523
